title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS company with 100+ employees across Tel Aviv and New York, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations reporting to the CRO. Requires 7+ years in RevOps, Sales Ops, or Strategy in high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, and strong cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. A hands-on leader-doer role building flows, dashboards, and data models while collaborating with globally distributed teams including the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Omri is a perfect match: 15+ years B2B SaaS experience well exceeds the 7+ year requirement, Fintech/SaaS background aligns with cloud cost management domain, Head of Revenue Operations title matches exactly, and Tel Aviv HQ location is ideal. Only minor gap is no explicit mention of $30M+ ARR scaling experience in profile, but seniority level suggests this.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I noticed Finout is building out RevOps at scale from a Tel Aviv base—exactly where my 15+ years of B2B SaaS experience intersects with your growth stage. I've spent my career architecting Salesforce-driven revenue systems and cross-functional GTM strategies for high-growth companies, and I'm particularly drawn to the fintech/FinOps space where data precision drives both sales efficiency and unit economics.

title: Head of Revenue Operations

Remote-first policy and scale (50+ AEs) align perfectly with his senior executive background and location flexibility.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've been tracking Hightouch's expansion and your approach to data activation resonates with how I've built RevOps functions at scale. With 15+ years connecting marketing, sales, and finance in high-velocity B2B environments, I'm interested in how you're thinking about RevOps infrastructure as you scale the AE org—particularly around pipeline forecasting and partnership channel ops at the $1M+ deal level.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong seeks a RevOps Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations to partner with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory composition and coverage. Responsibilities include redesigning territory models for global scalability, analyzing performance and account data for segmentation, and maintaining territory mapping in CRM systems like Fullcast and Salesforce. Requires deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models, strong analytical skills with Excel, SQL, and BI tools (Tableau, Retool). Salary range $149,000-$175,000 plus benefits and equity.
score_reason: Strong alignment with RevOps core competencies (Salesforce, territory strategy, analytics, B2B SaaS GTM), though less emphasis on pipeline forecasting/CAC-LTV analysis than ideal. Gong's sales-ops focus and data-driven approach match Omri's executive background well.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I came across Gong's RevOps Manager opening and it caught my attention—territory strategy optimization and CRM system management have been central to scaling revenue orgs I've built over 15+ years in B2B SaaS. Your focus on global territory redesign aligns perfectly with work I've done modeling account segmentation and sales coverage models to drive predictable growth.

description: Own how Theator shows up in the market. Turn product and engineering outcomes into market momentum, demand, and qualified leads. Translate complex AI surgical platform capabilities into simple outcome-driven stories. Own channel strategy: LinkedIn, email, partnerships, communities, events. AI fluency required — actively use AI to create leverage. High autonomy, fast-moving startup.
score_reason: Strong match — Growth Marketing Lead at AI healthtech startup in Tel Aviv. High autonomy, AI-native, full-funnel ownership. Israel-based.
ai_opener: Omri here — turning complex product capabilities into clear market momentum is exactly what I do. At Theator specifically, I'm drawn to the challenge of making surgical AI impossible to ignore in a market where credibility and proof points are everything.
